description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and rounded front corners, featuring a rich brown wood grain finish accented by thin gold trim along the top and bottom edges. The front panel displays a mesh speaker grille flanked by two prominent circular dials, likely for tuning and volume control, and a horizontal frequency indicator beneath the grille. The radio is powered by a black electrical cord and plug extending from the rear, suggesting it is designed for indoor use. The sides and back are plain, showcasing the smooth wooden surface without additional features. The overall design evokes a classic mid-20th-century aesthetic, blending functionality with decorative appeal.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with gold trim, front dials, a mesh speaker grille, and a power cord. The design is classic and decorative, with a rich brown finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the mesh speaker grille, two circular dials, a frequency indicator, and gold trim.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the smooth wooden surface and the attached black power cord and plug.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wooden back with gold trim and the power cord emerging from the left.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the uninterrupted wooden surface and rounded front corner.
